title: Aureline collection-view typed filter-AST boundary vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the typed, serialisable filter AST every dense collection surface (search, review, logs, packages, work items, admin grids) reads when it persists, exports, shares, or re-resolves a filter across providers, saved views, deep links, exports, and CLI output. The AST is a tree of typed nodes; every combinator names exactly one filter_operator_class from the closed combinator set, every leaf names exactly one operator class plus a typed field source class and a typed literal handling class, and every node carries a filter_compatibility_class plus an unknown-node isolation state so cross-build / cross-provider re-resolution can degrade visibly rather than silently dropping a constraint. Raw filter literal bytes, raw query bodies, raw absolute paths, raw symbol bodies, raw URLs, and raw row text MUST NOT appear on any record emitted against this schema; the record carries opaque ids, typed vocabulary, redaction-aware reviewable labels (<= 1024 graphemes), and policy / freshness / drift state only. The eventual collection / search crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new operator class, pattern dialect, source class, field source class, literal handling class, compatibility class, denial reason, or audit-event id is additive-minor and bumps filter_ast_schema_version; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row.

filter_ast_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the filter-AST vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional schema hint carried on worked fixtures.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests / portability exports. Raw filter literal bytes, raw field names, raw query bodies, raw absolute paths, and raw URLs never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries presentation-only summary copy; never carries raw filter literal bytes, raw field names, or raw row text.

running_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque ref to the running build identity the artifact was minted under. Pinned so a re-resolution can compare current build vs captured build for drift labelling.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. Re-validated on every reopen / re-resolution; a stale policy epoch denies with policy_epoch_expired.

surface_class.description: Closed surface-family vocabulary for collection-bearing surfaces. A filter AST is always minted by exactly one surface class.

filter_operator_class.description: Frozen filter-operator vocabulary. Combinators (combinator_all_of, combinator_any_of, combinator_none_of, combinator_not) wrap zero or more child nodes (combinator_not wraps exactly one). Every other operator is a leaf operator and references a typed field source class and operator-appropriate operand.

pattern_dialect_class.description: Closed pattern-dialect vocabulary. A node whose operator is matches_pattern or does_not_match_pattern MUST name exactly one dialect; a missing dialect denies with filter_pattern_dialect_unspecified.

filter_source_class.description: Closed filter-source vocabulary. The source class travels at the AST root and may also be set per node when a sub-tree is owned by a different source than its parent.

filter_field_source_class.description: Closed field-source vocabulary. provider_only_field referenced under client_local_filter denies with filter_field_unresolvable_in_local_source.

filter_literal_handling_class.description: Closed sensitive-literal handling vocabulary. literal_redacted_at_boundary_default is the default for every literal that crosses the schema boundary; the raw literal lives in the local store only and never leaves it. A literal-disclosing class without explicit user opt-in denies with filter_literal_export_requires_explicit_opt_in.

filter_compatibility_class.description: Closed AST-compatibility vocabulary. compatible_current is admissible only when every node, dialect, and field resolves under the current build; forward_compatible_unknown_operator_isolated permits an unknown operator within an isolatable subtree; the two incompatible classes refuse to resolve.

unknown_node_isolation_state.description: Whether an unknown operator / field at this node is isolated under a deny-closed combinator subtree (isolated) or escapes the subtree (not_isolatable).

literal_value_ref.description: Opaque reference to a literal value held in the local-only literal store. Raw literal bytes never live on this object.

value_set_ref.description: Opaque reference to a literal value set held in the local-only literal store.

range_ref.description: Opaque reference to a numeric / ordinal range held in the local-only literal store.

time_window_ref.description: Opaque reference to a time window expressed in the time-window registry.

tag_ref.description: Opaque reference to a tag in the tag registry.

filter_field_ref.description: Opaque reference to the field on the surface's row schema. Raw field names never appear; the registry resolves the opaque ref to the typed field.

filter_node_id.description: Stable, opaque id for a node within the AST. Allows audit / drift tooling to reference a specific node without depending on traversal order.

filter_node_compatibility.description: Per-node compatibility result. A node whose compatibility class is incompatible_* and whose isolation_state is not_isolatable forces the AST root to one of the two incompatible_* classes.

allOf.description: Combinator nodes (combinator_all_of, combinator_any_of, combinator_none_of) wrap a child list and MUST NOT name a field_ref or operand.
combinator_not MUST wrap exactly one child node and MUST NOT name a field_ref or operand.
Pattern operators MUST name exactly one pattern_dialect_class.

Equality, substring, and ordinal-comparison leaf operators (other than within_range / outside_range / within_time_window / nullability / tag operators) MUST carry a field_ref and a single literal_ref.
Set-membership operators MUST carry a field_ref and a single value_set_ref.
Range operators MUST carry a field_ref and a range_ref.
within_time_window MUST carry a field_ref and a time_window_ref.
Nullability operators MUST carry a field_ref only.
Tag operators MUST carry a field_ref and a tag_ref.

filter_node.description: One node in the filter AST. The schema enforces operator / operand pairing; the AST owner enforces tree-shape, cycle detection, and root-resolves-to-one-source-class invariants.

nodes.description: Flat list of every node in the AST, keyed by node_id and parent-keyed via child_node_ids. The root_node_id MUST appear exactly once in the list.

translated_provider_filter_ast_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider-translated filter AST id when root_source_class is provider_translated_filter or composed_local_over_provider; null otherwise.

policy_narrowed_subtree_node_ids.description: Subtree roots that are policy-narrowed pinned (read-only on the user surface). Empty when no policy narrowing applies.

saved_view_pinned_subtree_node_ids.description: Subtree roots pinned by a saved view (read-only on consumer surfaces). Empty when no saved view pins a subtree.

allOf.description: ast_compatibility_class compatible_current MUST imply every per-node compatibility class is compatible_current.
Translated provider filter AST is required when root_source_class is provider_translated_filter.

filter_ast_record.description: Durable filter AST. The eventual collection / search crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this record is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Raw filter literal bytes, raw query bodies, raw URLs, and raw absolute paths never appear on this record.

filter_ast_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event id vocabulary on the filter-AST stream.

filter_ast_denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary on the filter-AST stream. Denials fail closed; silent compatibility downgrades are forbidden.

filter_ast_audit_event_record.description: Filter-AST audit event. Raw filter literal bytes, raw field names, and raw query bodies never appear on this record.
